title: AI signals are starting to diverge across the ecosystem
author: topicspace
selftext:
The AI story just broke into pieces.

A few days ago it looked like a clean “cooling” trend across infrastructure.

Now it’s not one story anymore:

* Meta is suddenly accelerating
* NVIDIA is splitting — partnerships rising while other signals break down
* Intel’s bounce didn’t hold
* Attention is shifting away from the model layer (OpenAI / Anthropic)

So instead of “AI up or down,” parts of the system are moving in different directions.

[I’ve been mapping how this shifts in real time](http://topicspace.com) — here’s what it looks like right now:

https://preview.redd.it/jxu9q8la2fqg1.png?width=1270&format=png&auto=webp&s=f0cb85fab67907dda9f87adda1850885be0c109a

Curious if others are seeing the same fragmentation or reading it differently.

comments.author: topicspace
comments.body:
The part I’m still trying to understand:

Why is PLTR the only thing holding up while everything else (chips, cloud, model layer) is cooling?

Feels like a different demand driver entirely (gov / enterprise vs infra buildout), but not sure if that’s the full story.

Curious how others are thinking about that piece.
